headline: Chip Surge Drives Broad Rally as Geopolitical Jitters Fade to the Margin
body_html:
Thursday's session belonged to semiconductors, and it wasn't particularly close. The chip complex surged 4.19% on the day, pulling the broader market with it as buyers flooded into everything from automotive silicon to intellectual-property licensors. Breadth confirmed the conviction: 293 names in our universe closed higher against just 102 decliners, a ratio that speaks to genuine participation rather than a handful of mega-caps doing the lifting.

The geopolitical backdrop remained unsettled — Iranian forces claimed strikes on U.S. military targets in the Gulf, and the nation buried its slain supreme leader — yet equity markets largely absorbed the headlines. Energy was the lone sector in the red, off 1.01%, with oil-and-gas names and royalty plays among the session's steeper decliners. Every other sector finished positive, led by chips, software, and homebuilders, suggesting investors viewed the Middle East escalation as a risk to crude rather than a reason to de-risk equities broadly.

Allegro MicroSystems stood out as the session's most dramatic mover, rallying 11.49% to top all gainers, with Arm Holdings and Onto Innovation close behind at 9.2% and 8.83% respectively. The pattern is instructive: the day's leaders spanned automotive chips, IP licensing, and semiconductor equipment — a sign that the semiconductor bid was wide rather than concentrated in a single niche.

briefs.headline: Allegro MicroSystems Leads All Comers With Double-Digit Gain
briefs.body: Allegro MicroSystems surged 11.49% Thursday, the sharpest advance in our entire coverage universe. The automotive-semiconductor specialist attracted heavy buying interest on a day when chip-adjacent names broadly outperformed. Arm Holdings and Onto Innovation followed closely, rising 9.2% and 8.83% respectively, cementing the semiconductor equipment and IP subsectors as Thursday's clear center of gravity.

briefs.headline: Iran Strikes and a State Funeral Keep Energy Markets on Edge
briefs.body: Iranian forces claimed to have hit U.S. military targets in the Gulf, while the country held burial rites for its supreme leader, keeping geopolitical risk firmly on traders' radar. Prediction-market participants on Kalshi moved to price higher gasoline costs for an extended period in response to the escalating U.S.-Iran standoff. Energy was Thursday's only losing sector, declining 1.01%.

briefs.headline: Fed's Williams Sees Energy Prices Cooling Despite Mideast Conflict
briefs.body: New York Fed President John Williams offered a measured counterpoint to the day's war-risk headlines, indicating he expects energy price pressures to ease even as hostilities in the Middle East intensify. The remarks helped temper rate-path anxiety and likely contributed to the market's resilience. Fed officials continue to watch the conflict's commodity-price pass-through closely.

briefs.headline: Goldman Sachs Lands $70 Billion in Asset-Management Mandates
briefs.body: Goldman Sachs secured roughly $70 billion in new asset-management business through deals struck with Verizon and Lockheed Martin, in what ranks as a landmark institutional-client win for the firm. The agreements underscore large corporates' appetite to consolidate investment relationships with bulge-bracket managers. Details on mandate structure and fee arrangements were not disclosed.

briefs.headline: Palo Alto Chief Flags Unsustainable AI Token Costs
briefs.body: Palo Alto Networks CEO Nikesh Arora argued publicly that artificial-intelligence pricing must come down by roughly 90% as the cost of AI tokens continues to climb sharply. His comments add a prominent enterprise-technology voice to a growing debate about whether current AI infrastructure economics are viable at scale. The remarks resonated in software circles, where margins tied to AI inference are under scrutiny.

briefs.headline: Anthropic Taps Ben Bernanke for Independent Trust Role
briefs.body: AI startup Anthropic named former Federal Reserve Chair Ben Bernanke to its newly formed independent trust, signaling a push to bolster institutional credibility around the company's safety governance structure. Bernanke's appointment brings one of the most recognizable names in economic policy into the increasingly high-stakes world of frontier AI oversight. Terms of the role were not detailed.
